title: Koyfin Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)

meta_description: Koyfin Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). Koyfin (4.2★, Free). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using Koyfin — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is Koyfin?

Koyfin is a Bloomberg-style financial data terminal designed specifically for retail investors, combining institutional-grade charting, fundamental analysis tools, and macroeconomic data into a single platform. Founded in 2016 and headquartered in New York, Koyfin serves fundamental analysts, macro investors, and financial advisors who need deep market intelligence without the $24,000+ annual cost of Bloomberg Terminal. With a 4.2/5 rating and pricing starting at free, Koyfin delivers genuine Bloomberg-competitor functionality across stocks, ETFs, forex, crypto, and fixed-income instruments globally. Traders choose Koyfin specifically for its combination of institutional-quality fundamental data, customizable dashboards, and a legitimately useful free tier that doesn't artificially cripple your workflow.

How to Create Your Koyfin Account

Getting started with Koyfin takes less than five minutes. Here's exactly what you need to do:

  • Go to Koyfin's website and click the Sign Up button in the top right. You'll see options to register with email, Google, or Apple ID—pick whichever you use most to avoid password fatigue.
  • Provide your email address if you're not using social login. Enter your name, create a password (12+ characters recommended), and confirm. Koyfin doesn't require a phone number or extensive personal data for the free tier, which is refreshing compared to many fintech platforms.
  • Check your email for a verification link from Koyfin. Click it within 24 hours to activate your account. If it lands in spam, whitelist noreply@koyfin.com.
  • Log in and start immediately. Unlike competitors like Bloomberg Terminal (which requires enterprise approval) or thinkorswim (which ties you to TD Ameritrade), Koyfin gives you full platform access within seconds. No lengthy verification calls or account minimums.
  • Optional: Connect your broker if you want portfolio tracking. Koyfin integrates with major brokers but doesn't execute trades—it's a data and analysis platform, not an order-routing system.

Estimated setup time: 3–5 minutes from start to first chart.

Setting Up Koyfin for the First Time

Dashboard Layout

When you first log in, you'll see a customizable dashboard grid. The left sidebar contains your watchlists, alerts, and saved analyses. The center workspace is where you build your personalized layout—charts, data tables, news feeds, and screeners all live here as draggable widgets. Unlike static platforms, Koyfin's dashboard is genuinely flexible; you can resize widgets by dragging corners and rearrange them in seconds. Start by creating a watchlist for the assets you follow most closely. Click "Create Watchlist," name it (e.g., "Tech Stocks," "Macro Indicators"), and add tickers by searching or copy-pasting them.

Configure Your Primary Chart

Drag a Chart widget onto your dashboard. Search for a ticker—let's say AAPL. Once loaded, you'll see Koyfin's chart interface: timeframe selector (1M to 5Y), overlay tools (moving averages, Bollinger Bands, volume), and indicator library. Right-click any chart element to access settings. For fundamental analysis (Koyfin's strength), open the Fundamentals panel on the right—you'll see revenue, earnings, margins, and debt data stretching back 10+ years. This is where Koyfin outshines TradingView; it's built for long-term analysis, not just short-term technicals.

Set Up News and Alerts

Add a News Feed widget and filter by your watchlist symbols. Enable notifications for price-based alerts: right-click any watchlist item, select "Set Alert," and specify a price level or percentage move. These trigger email notifications and in-app alerts—crucial for catching earnings announcements or macro events that move your holdings.

Save Your First Analysis

Once your dashboard is arranged, save it: click the Menu button and select "Save Layout." Give it a name like "Daily Review" and save. You can create multiple layouts—one for macro tracking, one for deep dives on individual stocks, one for watching sectors. Switch between them using the Layout selector in the top menu.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Alerts

Koyfin's alerts system watches price levels, percentage moves, and custom conditions across your entire watchlist. Set a price alert on NVDA at $120, and Koyfin emails you the second it hits that level. You can also create news-based alerts: for example, "notify me when earnings season hits semiconductor stocks." The free tier includes 50 active alerts; Pro and Premier tiers expand that to 500+ and unlimited. Unlike TradingView's alert system, Koyfin's alerts are server-side (not browser-dependent), so you'll get notified even if you're offline.

2. Performance Analytics

This feature synthesizes your portfolio's performance against benchmarks. If you connect your broker account (Koyfin supports most major platforms), it automatically tracks your holdings, calculates position-level returns, and compares your performance to the S&P 500, NASDAQ, or custom benchmarks. This is particularly valuable for advisors managing multiple client portfolios—you can filter by asset class, sector, or time period. Premium tiers unlock attribution analysis, showing which positions drove your returns.

3. Macro Dashboard

Koyfin's macro data is its biggest differentiator versus thinkorswim or other retail platforms. Drag a Macro widget onto your dashboard and access global economic indicators in real-time: US unemployment, inflation, GDP, Fed policy rates, yield curves across countries, housing starts, and dozens more. Each indicator has a 10+ year historical chart. Filter by country and data category. This is essential context for macro investors and fundamental analysts tracking macro headwinds.

4. News Feed

Koyfin aggregates financial news from 100+ sources and tags each story by asset, sector, and topic. Filter your feed to show only news relevant to your watchlist—say, all stories mentioning your semiconductor holdings. The free tier shows delayed news; Pro and Premier tiers include real-time news feeds from major outlets. You can also create custom news alerts for specific keywords or themes.

5. Mobile App

Koyfin's iOS and Android apps sync your dashboards from the web version. You can view your watchlists, receive alerts, and check macro indicators on the go. The mobile interface is simplified compared to the web platform (no custom widget building), but it's functional for checking positions and responding to alerts. The app is included in all tiers.

6. Screeners

Use Koyfin's stock screener to filter securities by fundamental criteria: P/E ratio, dividend yield, debt-to-equity, revenue growth, insider ownership, and 50+ other metrics. Save screening criteria as templates and run them daily. For example, create a "Dividend Aristocrats" screen (20+ consecutive years of dividend increases, yield > 2%) and it'll update automatically as new earnings data arrives.

7. Customizable Watchlists

Beyond basic asset lists, Koyfin's watchlists display custom data columns: latest price, 52-week high/low, P/E ratio, dividend yield, debt, float shares, insider ownership percentage. Sort by any column and color-code rows by performance. This is invaluable for side-by-side comparison of company fundamentals—exactly what retail investors using Google Sheets or Excel struggle with.

Koyfin Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Free Tier ($0/month)

Includes real-time data for US stocks, daily-delayed data for other markets, 50 active alerts, basic charting, fundamental data back 10 years, and macro indicators. This is legitimately useful for casual investors and students learning to analyze stocks. Trade-off: you're on a 15-minute delay for most international data, and screeners are limited to 100 results. No portfolio syncing.

Plus Tier ($25/month)

Adds real-time data for forex and crypto, international stock real-time data (not delayed), 200 active alerts, and portfolio tracking with performance analytics. If you follow crypto or international markets, this is the entry point where Koyfin becomes complete. The portfolio syncing alone justifies the cost for most traders managing multiple holdings.

Pro Tier ($55/month)

Includes real-time news feeds (instead of delayed), 500 active alerts, advanced charting tools (drawing tools, order block detection), white-label customization, and API access. This is where advisors and serious individual investors sit. The API unlocks automation: you can feed Koyfin data into other applications or create custom workflows. The real-time news feed is a substantial upgrade for day-traders and active traders.

Premier Tier ($119/month)

The top tier adds unlimited alerts, dedicated support, advanced analytics features, and institutional-grade data coverage (including fixed-income securities detailed analysis). If you're managing significant assets or advising clients, this tier's institutional features and support justify the cost. Most retail traders never need this level.

Recommendation by Trader Type: Begin with the Free tier if you're just learning. Move to Plus ($25) if you own crypto, trade forex, or follow international stocks. Pro ($55) if you trade actively or manage others' money. Premier ($119) only if you're building an advisory business.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of Koyfin

  • Use macro data as a filter, not a signal alone. Koyfin's economic indicators are comprehensive, but don't buy or sell based on a single unemployment number. Instead, use the macro dashboard to understand the macro regime (hiking cycle, recession risk, stagflation risk) and filter your stock picks accordingly. For example, when yield curves invert (easily visible on Koyfin), avoid high-debt companies and favor quality stocks—let macroeconomic context guide your stock selection.
  • Save multiple layouts for different analysis modes. Create separate dashboards: one for daily watchlist checks, one for deep fundamental research (heavy on balance sheet data), one for macro monitoring, one for earning-season analysis. Switching between layouts takes one click and keeps you focused on the task at hand.
  • Combine Fundamentals with Technicals for entry timing. Koyfin's strength is fundamental data, but it has solid technical tools too. Identify stocks that pass your fundamental screens (high growth, low debt, competitive advantages), then use Koyfin's technical overlays to time entries: wait for support bounces, moving average breakouts, or RSI oversolds. This combines institutional-grade due diligence with retail-friendly technicals.
  • Set up earnings alerts before earnings season. Create a watchlist of companies you want to track through earnings and set price alerts 2-3% above and below current price, plus a custom news alert for "earnings" keyword on that watchlist. Koyfin will notify you the second earnings are released or announced, giving you first-mover advantage.
  • Use screeners to build thesis-driven portfolios, not just signals.** Rather than "find me all stocks above $100," use screeners to find specific stories: "consumer discretionary stocks with <0.5x debt, >15% EPS growth, insider ownership >10%." Save these templates and run them monthly. You're building repeatable investment theses, not hunting random signals.
  • Export watchlist data to Excel for deeper analysis. Koyfin doesn't include backtesting or complex modeling, but you can export watchlist data (price, fundamentals, ratios) to CSV and build your own analysis in Excel or Python. This is faster than copy-pasting from multiple sources and ensures data consistency.
  • Connect your broker for true portfolio tracking. If you use Fidelity, Schwab, Interactive Brokers, Webull, or another supported broker, sync it to Koyfin. The platform then automatically calculates your unrealized gains, dividend income, sector allocation, and performance vs. benchmarks. This single integration turns Koyfin from a research tool into your central portfolio dashboard.

Common Koyfin Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue: "Why are my international stock prices delayed on the Free tier?"

Koyfin delays international data by 15–20 minutes on the Free plan to keep costs low. This is by design; real-time international data is expensive to license. Fix: upgrade to Plus ($25/month) for real-time international data, or use the Free tier for US-focused research and accept the delay on other markets. If you actively trade international stocks, Plus is non-negotiable.

Issue: "My portfolio syncing shows outdated holdings."

Broker syncing can lag by a few hours if your broker's API is slow or if Koyfin's servers are catching up. Fix: manually refresh your connection in Settings > Connected Accounts, or wait 4–6 hours for automatic sync. If it's been 24 hours and your portfolio still shows old data, check that you haven't changed your broker's password (which breaks OAuth connections). Contact Koyfin support if the issue persists; they're responsive to Plus+ customers.

Issue: "The charting tools feel clunky compared to TradingView."

Koyfin prioritizes fundamental analysis and macro data; technical charting is secondary. Its drawing tools and indicator library are functional but less polished than TradingView's. Fix: use Koyfin for fundamental screening and macro context, then export ticker symbols to TradingView for detailed technical analysis. This hybrid approach is common among advanced traders and gives you the best of both platforms without paying $180/month for TradingView Pro.

Issue: "I want to backtest strategies but Koyfin doesn't support it."

Koyfin is explicitly a research and analytics platform, not a backtesting engine. This is a feature gap, not a bug. Fix: export price and fundamental data from Koyfin, then test your strategies in a backtesting framework like Backtrader, MetaTrader, or ThinkorSwim's Strategy Analyzer. Use Koyfin to identify candidates and validate fundamentals, then backtest your execution rules elsewhere.

Is Koyfin Worth It? Our Verdict

Koyfin is worth $25–55/month if you're a fundamental analyst, macro-focused investor, or advisor building investment theses—it delivers Bloomberg Terminal-quality data and dashboards at 1/100th the cost, with a genuinely useful free tier. The Platform shines for long-term investors and portfolio managers who need institutional data without institutional pricing; it's weak for day-traders (charting lags rivals, no backtesting) and those who want all-in-one execution (no broker integration or trading). Rating 4.2/5 reflects its niche excellence: elite for fundamental analysis and macro research, adequate but not best-in-class for technicals. Start free, upgrade to Plus if you follow crypto or international markets, move to Pro when you're trading actively or managing others' capital. Skip if you're a day-trader looking for a TradingView alternative, or if you need broker integration with one-click trading; use Interactive Brokers or Schwab's StreetSmart Edge instead. For everyone else hunting institutional data without institutional costs, Koyfin is the answer. See our full Koyfin review for detailed feature breakdowns and performance data.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
